summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that picklescan's _http_get function follows unvalidated HTTP 302 redirects to arbitrary hosts. A redirect server returns 302 pointing to a separate simulated-internal server, and picklescan follows the redirect, connects to the internal server, and processes its response as pickle data.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause picklescan to follow a 302 redirect from Server A (the user-requested URL) to Server B (a different host simulating an internal service), connect to Server B, process data from Server B, and detect a dangerous import (builtins.eval) in the data returned by Server B.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started two HTTPS servers inside the container, called scan_url against Server A, which returned a 302 redirect to Server B. picklescan followed the redirect, connected to Server B, and processed the pickle payload from Server B, detecting the builtins.eval dangerous import in the scan results.
verification_method: The exploit script tracks all HTTP requests received by both Server A (redirect) and Server B (target/internal). SSRF is confirmed by checking that (1) Server B received at least one request (proving the redirect was followed to a different host), and (2) the scan result includes the builtins.eval global that was embedded only in Server B's response (proving data from the internal host was processed).
evidence_produced_by_run: Server B (simulated internal service) received an HTTPS request, confirming the scanner followed the redirect to a different host.
The scan result includes a builtins.eval global detected in the data returned by Server B, confirming the internal host's response was processed.
Server A only received one request (the initial scan), while Server B received the redirected request, demonstrating cross-host redirect following.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref v0.0.34, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
